and the new president wipes out the number one retirement benefit for making you and your heirs wealthy with the stroke of an executive order patent. And we're going to use both of them, but which should be first? How many vote for one? Really? You vote for the first one? No! No, no, no! Everybody else either got it right or didn't vote. Oh, no, the second... No, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no. Most of the time you want to play your ace first. This is the ace. First of all, it's scarier than shit. Second of all, it's timely, right? So we're using current news, right? And we've played a different emotional card. The first one is how to make your money worth more. The second one is about you and your heirs. So if we're in a financial services business, who are we selling to for the most part? Boomers and seniors. And for the most part, if they got enough money to manage, they're pushing 60. And they start to care about what happens after they're gone. See, they're year 20. You don't give a crap what happens after you're gone. First of all, you don't think you're ever going to be gone. And beyond that, who cares, right? When you get to be like 68, people give you a sweater for Christmas. You return it and you put the money in a little piggy bank for your grandkids. That's how old people behave, right? So this really matters to them. So yeah, this goes first. Now it gets a little tougher when you got to make the decision on page 17, because there's four. Okay? So there's proven and vulnerable ironclad type of the day. Well, you can read it. The second, Congress is out to steal your thing. The third is how Congress plans to make your heirs pay for their inheritance. And you're already taxed retirement savings. And the last one is actually what is actually going on. So here finally we got to the actual situation, the death of the stretch IRA. There's new laws coming that are somehow going to screw all that up, right? So now if you have to make a choice, which of these are you going to lead with and put first? How many think B? How many think A? You should be sitting next to each other. How many think C? Yeah, you're right. Absolutely. The strongest card here is C, because it has more things in it. Right? It's the heirs trigger. It's anger. You've already paid taxes on this stuff. It's Congress, who everybody hates. They have the lowest approval rating. Incredibly. They have a lower approval rating with the public than either Hillary or Trump. I mean, you got to do something to have a 22% public approval rating. Dead Hitlers at 24. I mean, and Congress is at 22. I mean, so if you want to demonize somebody, they're it, right? Already taxed. They pisses you off, doesn't it? You've already paid taxes on it. That's not up here. It's not here. I got Congress here, but I don't have already taxed here. Right? It's all good, but it's still make their heirs pay for. So if you microanalyze how many little buttons you push, you're good to arrive at C. Well, it also solves that problem, right? Is you would think if you got one, you know what it, you know you got one and you know what it is. I wouldn't necessarily bet on that. I mean, you know, it is insider. It's industry terminology, right? Retirement savings. Now, if all you want is IRA holders, which is all he wants, then I have opened the doors for some crap to flow in that has to be shoveled back out the other side. That is of no value to me. But if I also get some good stuff that I wouldn't have got otherwise, it's worth shoveling the crap, right? So not only is this, you know, copy issues here, but now the what order? Because I'd use them all in any kind of a long presentation. But where do they belong? Right? That's the game. In absence of any other formula, this is a blue star. It's not in your book. In the absence of any other reason for a different formula, here's a stacking formula to use. A dark item, an embellishment, an even darker item, a darkest item, light at the end of the tunnel, light at the end of the tunnel, and then finally personal positive benefits. So on 18, Dave, that's exactly what I'm going to do. I'm going to do it, move on, and I'm going to come back to it right before launch is what I'm going to do. Okay? Okay. So Trump stacks items in every speech. So if you pay attention, he stacks them the same way every single time. On page 18, how does he stack these items? Almost every time he makes a reference to any of them, he makes a reference to all of them, and he stacks them in the same way every time. He doesn't have to be a proctor to do it. He does it the same way every time. For the sake of time, I'll give it to you. You can number them. They are pouring across our border as number one. Number two is about who they are, which is darker. We are getting the drug dealers, the criminals, the rapists, and Mexico is getting our money. That's second. Third, if we don't have borders, we don't have a country. That's darker still. Four, we're going to build a wall. Five, he now doesn't say it. He gets the audience to say it. And who's going to pay for the wall? And everybody goes, Mexico! He stacks them the same way virtually every time I've seen him do it. He stacks it in the exact same order. No randomness to this at all. Same with what's on page 19. Here's the items. Here's the stack. One goes next to, we have terrible trade deals. We're getting our butt kicked by everybody. We're not winning anymore. Two, companies are moving jobs too and then stick a place in. Sometimes he names every place. Sometimes he names the place that a company in the state he's in has moved a job to. If he's in Indiana, carriers moved their factory to Mexico. When he was in California, it's all about Apple getting your phones made by slave labor in China. But one or the other. By the way, there's not a liberal on earth who should be carrying a smart phone. Because they are being made by slave labor in China. Children, they had bars on those windows and the factories are real tight so the little kids can crawl out and jump. The third thing is I'm going to bring our jobs back from wherever. Sometimes he names every place. I'm going to bring our jobs back from China, Vietnam, Mexico. Sometimes he names the place that links to the place he just used. Fourth, I'm going to be our greatest jobs president ever. And then five, he gets, and that's more recent, he gets to his little wrap on, we're going to put America first. His latest incarnation of this started yesterday, which is for decades the Clintons have been putting America last and putting the Clintons first. I'm going to put America first. But it's still fifth in this little hierarchy. So he keeps tweaking the copy, but he hasn't changed the stack. Doesn't mean he won't, but it strongly suggests it's not accidental. It strongly suggests this is not accidental. Then the last one on page 20 is this stack. So what's number one? What's the first thing he does on this stack? So it's either or, that's right. It's either our airports are terrible, we're in a third world country. You can't believe I fly into, and then he names one that's a rat hole. And then I fly into Bahrain and there's, there's REIT, but you know, or he starts with our roads, our bridges, our cities, and then he does the airports. He pretty much does both every time and they're flippable. You're right. Third is, I'm going to be the greatest infrastructure president. Sometimes he says I'm going to be the greatest roads and bridges president. And usually then, who knows more about building stuff than I do. That's almost always fourth. And depending on time, you'll embellish that, right? I mean, or where he is, he'll talk about, you know, like he's in Washington, Virginia last night. So he talked about the post office building in Washington, because Washington and Virginia are really one place, but it's all, who knows more about building stuff than I do. Nobody. The post office, it's all going to be done early and it's all coming in under budget. Yada, yada, yada. Then sixth, he sticks in, he says you're going to be so proud. And then fill in the blank. You're going to be so proud of me. You're going to be so proud of America. You're going to be so proud of our airports. You're going to be so, then seventh, he gets to the hat. Right? We're going to make America great again. Sometimes now he's not doing that one either. He's doing this. So what are we going to do? Everybody yells, we're going to make America great again. They've already memorized the last one. That's his stack. It suggests, because he stacks it the same way every time this was not an accident. Now he may have got to it by accident. He may have tried it four or five different ways early. I haven't come back and looked, but it's locked in now. Right? And that's what you want to do, right? Especially when you're communicating with the same audience again and again. There should be an overarching way that you stack the things you want them to know and buy into that you stay with. However you change the flesh and the costume on the bones, the skeleton should stay the same. It should not be random or accidental. Number four, a lot of fun. So I'm going to talk about it a little bit, and then Dave is going to give you some more sort of demonstration on this. He is a practitioner of it. I'm only a thief of it for copywriting purposes. So number four is about coal read. If you do not know what this is, that's what the fortune tellers we had here, whether they were good or bad, I don't know, but they were basically living off of a coal read. So coal read is your deductions about someone either based purely on your skill of deduction or ideally cheating by getting some sort of information in advance, which got easier for people in this business today because everybody puts all their shit up on Facebook. So they used to have to go to much greater extremes. The guy in that book who sent me was having people stationed across the street from where people were lining up to come in and get their readings with big parabolic listing devices in order to overhear their conversation and then run around and slip some information. So if you see, what's the big criminal, if you see John Edwards or the fat woman who's on, you know, Dr. Phil once a while, who talked to the dead and they help you communicate with your dead aunt or whatever, they're doing a coal read on you. That's what they're doing. And in many cases, because they're one to one, they get to like experiment. So they can say, there's an older gentleman and if there's no reaction to your, in your eyes, oh no, I meant maybe it's an older woman and now your eyes, right? So you just told them and they were wrong, but you don't, it's okay because they were right on the second try, right? So... Yeah. Yeah, yeah, but yeah, yeah, so it's not all hits in a live before, but like he's, he's on the road doing live shows. Two times ago when I was in Atlantic City, he had to place Pac, by the way, 800 people at whatever the ticket price was, who were all there hoping he's going to pick on them and help them communicate with their dead dog or their dead aunt or their dead, you know, whatever. And now again, there's a lot, there's preexisting, you know, tendency to believe. So most people go to that show because they believe that can happen. That helps. They desperately want to believe because they desperately... So Houdini totally disbelieved this and spent the last 10 years of his life debunking all this stuff and yet at the same time he kept trying to find somebody who was real because he very much wanted to talk to his dead mama. And so people want to believe this stuff. And, you know, they had, they were here for just entertainment, right? And most of you took it as that. But if somebody goes to their building and pulls up in a parking lot with a psychic sign on it and goes in and buys a psychic reading, nine out of 10 times, they either believe or they really want to believe. And so the cold read can even be a little clumsy and it still is okay, right? So the cold read is very powerful and it can be moved into what we do in copy and it should be a lot. So page 22 are a couple of basic examples, okay? Now these are actual cold read scripts that the person in the booth here... I forget how long we've been here. Wednesday night, the person in the booth... It's all a blur, by the way, after about halfway through the second day. I don't know. In 40 years of this, I have no idea. But it is Wednesday night, wasn't it? Yeah. So the ones that were here Wednesday night, this is stuff they might have used. This is right out of cold read scripts. But it could easily be moved to copy. And these are like fail-safe scripts because they play both sides of the coin. And you let it be a hit, which with ever side works, right? I see you are at a time of your life when certain new decisions are in order. Who isn't? Right? I haven't yet decided what I'm going to eat for dinner tonight. I honestly don't know. And I'm not sure if I'm going to go to the track and watch my horse race, or go straight home because the dog will have been alone all day. I've got to figure this out by the time we're done today. Son of a bitch, that person knew I was working on that. Amazing. Amazing! Right? Amazing! You are at a kind of a... So we're not going to say you're at a crossroads. You are at a kind of a crossroads and need to make a choice soon. What's soon? It's whatever. Right? We want it to be. Next one. You are usually, not always, a person of strong character and determination. Yet you are at times a wimp. Both sides of the coin. The person reading it or hearing it, in this case, gloms on to whichever side of that is right. Ignores whichever side of that is wrong. Right? And there's all... It's all weasel language that sometimes leads to yada yada. Here are what are called Barnum statements on page 23. So these are cold read statements that almost everybody will agree with. This is, by the way, also astrological forecast stuff. See, it doesn't matter, by the way, whether you do it with tarot cards or red, white, blue, poker chips, none of that crap matters. This is what's being done. You have a need for people to like and admire you. It's important to you. Who doesn't? And obviously this could lead into a pitch for, I mean, we could sell, how to win friends and influence people right off the back of that statement. Right? You have a tendency to be critical of yourself. Who doesn't? Well, Trump. But, I mean... But I mean, it's a short... Not the most introspective human on the planet. But most people are, at least in some ways, self-critical. There's something about you you don't really like. You wish you had hair. You know, somebody next to you would not much, wishes they had the courage to just shave it all off and go... I mean, there's something we know like our nose. We don't like the fact that we don't know a foreign language. We wish we knew more about wine because we go out to dinner once a month with this couple and he's a real wine asshole and we're sick. Right? So, I mean, there's something, right? You have a tendency to be critical of yourself. You have a great deal of unused capability which you have not turned to your advantage. Now, this sentence, by the way, this is a sentence that is like... There's a story about the psychic who goes to the king and says, all your friends are going to be dead and you're going to finish out your life all alone and he kills the guy and the next guy comes and says, you are in such great health and such a terrific physical specimen and you are going to outlive all your friends and he gives that guy a million bucks. I mean, it's the same thing, right? You have a great deal of unused capability which you have not turned to your advantage which means you're a sloth, right? You are frittering away your life and your capability and you're a lazy schmuck but that's not going to be well received. Then there's some more reverse barnums that take both sides of the coin. So, these get extended. You have a tendency to be critical of yourself yet you can at times be bold and outspoken and have others turn to you for leadership. Now, what's significant about almost all of them is that they are telling people something they like hearing about themselves and then using that. So, all of that can migrate right into copy. You can read this on your own. I'll give you a couple of examples and what is this from? This is from Wind Trillians. So, this is selling people on why they ought to gamble on the lottery. So, if you look at page 24, you will see barnum statements. You'll see a cold read. I believe you are the kind of person who secretly dreams of a big windfall of cash. This is a very safe cold read in this circumstance because this person has come to a website called Wind Trillians. They have logged on to a website called Wind Trillians. It is about playing the lottery. I do not have to have any psychic capability whatsoever to say, I believe you are the kind of person who's secret and ain't much of a secret who secretly dreams of a big windfall of cash. The person goes, wow. Life altering money that can change everything. You long for freedom, adventure, maybe world travel. So, how safe a cold read is that? The over...it's what... So, travel giveaways are the most popular giveaways of all. Always have been, always are. Nothing works better than free trip to Vegas, free cruise, free whatever. Nothing works better. The prizes everybody wants when they go on a game show is they want to go somewhere. There used to be a show before you were born called The Dating Game, a very popular show. So, the Bachelor and the Bachelor at it are sort of the dating game extended and made really sleazy. But the dating game was sleazy enough. So, one person of some gender would be behind a wall. So, let's say it's a guy, it's you. And there would be...was it three? Yes, so it would be three women who all now get to ask you questions. They can't see you. But they get to ask you questions and you answer. And then they decide whether they're going to go on a date with you or not. And the date is like a prize. So, it's like you get to go spend a weekend with somebody you just met and ask five questions on the show. And the show's chaperone goes with you to make sure you don't have any fun in case the date works out. But it's probably going to be torture. But that was the entertainment, right? And the reason people put themselves through all this was the free trip. That's why people put themselves through this. So, this is a very safe cold read. Maybe world travel, right? Then, so they don't feel ashamed of themselves for responding to this appeal, I've put in, but probably also the ability to help others that you can't afford to help now. And actually, look, most people, if they hit the lottery, they spend some small sum of it, just like the football player who gets the contract, they all buy a house for their mother. Now, they also buy six Lamborghinis and, you know, a year's supply of Coke and, yeah. But, I mean, they usually go buy a house for their mother, right? You are not a freeloader at all. See, this is now, if you go back to yesterday's list, this is making it okay to try and win the lottery. You're not a freeloader. You've worked hard. You've paid your dues. You've been a model citizen. You may still work hard now. You're an honest, ethical, productive person. The criminal in the world says, I'm an honest, ethical, productive person. All right? Yet, you've found it very hard to ever get very far ahead financially. Safe cold read. They're at a site called Win Trillions. In my opinion, you deserve a big break. Who doesn't think they deserve a big break? All right? What week aren't you walking around thinking? You know, it should be easier by now. Damn it. Right? One day, just one stinking day, everything ought to fall in place. You know, I deserve it. I'm kind of animals. I slow down when there's an old person. If I'm right about you. So this is all cold read copy. It's right out of cold read scripts. So it's right out of a swipe file of cold read scripts and Barnum statements. Here's some more. Starting on page 25. Now we, now here's like from your list yesterday, here's a little transparency. You were invited. We reached out to you. You've been chosen. Now, if you stop there, it's not really believable. Right? So I explain it. Frankly, we do a lot of what is called data mining. We obtain and evaluate information about people. To find those most deserving of an outstretched hand of goodwill, they lift up in life at the time they are most ready and likely to accept it. Now that's a translation for, we do a lot of data mining to find people who are, we skipped over it yesterday, who are highly susceptible right now to you should play the lottery. But we don't say it that way because that's a little too much transparency. Right? We do a great deal of research. Right? We're sure so they don't get rattled. We never share our information with anybody. So we know certain things about you. The whole next paragraphs and other, it's more cold read stuff. For one thing, we know you are kind and generous almost to a fault. Is that a safe cold read line? Who could you say it to who would disagree with it? That's me. And everybody has somebody in their life, they're being generous to a fault too. Everybody. You are neither greedy or selfish. Who's going to say I'm a greedy selfish son of a, nope, you got me right. This is amazing. Right? We make it okay again. Of course, we want you to enrich yourself and raise yourself yada, yada, yada. Familiar. They all know Harry Potter, JK Rowling, I pulled a familiarity thing to use. So this is a lot of, and we won't go through the other, but I gave you two more pages. This is a lot of cold read copy worked into this sales line. I get to, and I have to eventually get to the thing. Here's how this works. You sign up, you get a free membership in the Wind Trillions Club, which allows you to have access to all the lotteries of the world. You can buy one ticket, you can buy ten tickets. I got to get to all that, right? And I got to get to all the features and benefits. You don't have to go down to the convenience store in the rain with a bunch of cash in your pocket and worry about getting mugged. You do it all online. You can make sure you never forget your numbers because you can lock in and all. I got to get to all that, but I get to it along with, how long is the main VSL? It's 52 minutes. Now we may have to scale it back. I don't know yet, but we're starting at 52 minutes. Where's the split about two-thirds in when I start doing product? No, we just start at, yeah, but there's one minute to get to the end. Okay, so 32 minutes of that is this stuff. It's literally the VSL sitting across from you in the little booth out there with the crystal ball and the tarot cards talking to you about you. That's what it is. And then the last 20 minutes are, okay, here's how this thing works. Isn't that cool? Now, we may have to scale it, I don't know, 52 minutes is long. But by the way, back to modus operandi, I never write to a length or a page count. I write to maximum effectiveness. Now, if I now feel I know I've gone too far, but who the hell would sit down to start with and say, I'm going to write 70 pages about a sinus pill? Nobody, right? And no client would let him. The client would come in the door and say, I want a 16-page sales letter, I want a reply card, and I want an order from him. That's what the client would say. No client's going to come in the door and say, I want 77 pages about a sinus pill. It doesn't happen. Trust me. It's a fight. No client comes in and says, I want a 52-minute VSL about buying these lottery tickets. You know what he wanted? A better landing page. That's what he wanted. This is what he's winded up with. Right? He probably doesn't even like it. I don't care. And now we'll start here. And now we know when they bail out, right? So if I have to fix this, I'll fix it. But the person most susceptible to this is OK with almost any length. So if we can fix that part of the problem and we can drive the most susceptible people to it, we're going to be fine. We're going to interrupt it. We're going to come back to it right before lunch with Dave's stuff. Hijack number five. Answering the real objection. So if you have huge...
